Implement the following plan: # Onboarding Flow Design Overhaul ## Context The signup/login pages have a polished editorial design (warm cream, gold accents, Playfair Display typography, morphing blobs). But the **profile setup modal** uses a cold dark navy (`rgb(4,20,29)`) with no CSS variables, and the **welcome message** is a plain white card with a generic party popper SVG. These three consecutive screens feel like three different apps. This plan harmonizes them into one cohesive editorial flow. ## Files to Modify | File | Change | |------|--------| | `client/src/components/HomePage/home.css` | Restyle profile editor: replace hardcoded colors with CSS variables, match auth page design | | `client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx` | Update title/copy, add placeholders, color-coded username feedback | | `client/src/components/WelcomeMessage/welcomemessage.css` | Full rewrite: CSS variables, dark mode, remove duplicate @media blocks | | `client/src/components/WelcomeMessage/WelcomeMessage.jsx` | Replace party popper with pen icon, add "Begin writing" CTA, update copy | ## Change 1: Profile Setup Modal (home.css + Home.jsx) ### CSS — Replace hardcoded navy with warm editorial theme - `.profile-editor`: `rgb(4,20,29)` -> `var(--bg-card)` + `var(--border-card)` + `var(--shadow-modal)`. Change from `width:50%; height:90%` to `max-width:440px; height:auto` (matches auth-form-card pattern) - `.profile-editor-bg`: `rgba(141,141,141,0.2)` -> `var(--bg-backdrop-heavy)`, increase blur to 8px - `.edit-profile-title-container`: Add `font-family: "Playfair Display"`, use `var(--text-heading)` - `.save-bttn` / `.save-bttn-disabled`: Match `.primary-button` from signup (dark bg, hover lift, disabled opacity) - `.edit-profile-name-input` / `.edit-profile-bio-input`: Match `.auth-input` from signup — `var(--bg-primary)`, `var(--border-card)`, gold focus ring (`box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(212,168,83,0.12)`) - `.input-title`: Match label style from signup — uppercase, `var(--text-muted)`, `0.7rem` - `.input-title-container`: Change to `justify-content: space-between` (label left, counter right) - `.name-input-container` / `.bio-input-container`: Remove border wrapper, use gap layout - `.profile-edit-container`: Remove hardcoded navy background - Add `@keyframes slideUp` (same as signup.css) for the card entrance - Remove hardcoded `color: rgb(255,255,255) !important` from mobile @media blocks - Add username status classes: `.username-status-available` (green), `.username-status-taken` (red), `.username-status-checking` (muted) - Add dark mode overrides for elevated inputs ### JSX — Welcoming copy + username feedback - Title: "Edit profile" -> "Welcome to Iskrib" - Add subtitle: `

Set up your writing identity.

` - Add `placeholder="Your name"` on name input - Add `placeholder="A few words about you..."` on bio textarea - Username status: apply color classes + prepend checkmark/X unicode chars - Save button: show "Saving..." text during upload, disable during upload ## Change 2: Welcome Message (welcomemessage.css + WelcomeMessage.jsx) ### CSS — Full rewrite - `.welcome-message-container`: `position: fixed`, `var(--bg-backdrop-heavy)`, blur 6px - `.welcome-message-child-container`: `var(--bg-card)`, `var(--border-card)`, `var(--shadow-modal)`, `max-width: 400px`, `border-radius: 16px` - Remove the 54px square close icon -> small 32px circular close button top-right (`.welcome-close-btn`) - Add `.welcome-heading`: Playfair Display, `var(--text-heading)` - `.welcome-message`: Lora serif, italic, `var(--text-body)` - `.iskrib-word`: Playfair Display, `var(--accent-amber)`, bold - Add `.welcome-cta`: Full-width button matching `.primary-button` (dark bg, hover lift) - Add `.welcome-icon`: `var(--accent-amber)` for the pen icon - Dark mode: gold CTA button, stronger shadow - **Remove duplicate @media blocks** (lines 56-77 and 79-100 were identical) - Single responsive block at 480px ### JSX — Editorial design - Replace 80px party popper SVG (100+ lines) with 48px Material pen/edit icon (single path, `currentColor`) - Add heading: `

You're all set

` - Update message: "Your thoughts now have a home on **Iskrib**. The blank page awaits." - Add CTA: `` (calls `onClose`) - Add small X close button top-right (aria-labeled) - Confetti colors: brand palette `['#D4A853', '#C4943E', '#E0BA6A', '#FAF9F6', '#8A9E7A']` instead of rainbow - Softer animation: `scale: 0.95, y: 12` initial instead of `scale: 0` (gentle reveal vs popping from nothing) ## Visual Consistency After Changes | Screen | Background | Card | Typography | Accent | |--------|-----------|------|------------|--------| | Signup | `--bg-primary` | `--bg-card` + `--shadow-card-sm` | Playfair + Lora + Outfit | Gold focus rings | | Profile Setup | `--bg-backdrop-heavy` | `--bg-card` + `--shadow-modal` | Playfair + Lora + Outfit | Gold focus rings | | Welcome | `--bg-backdrop-heavy` | `--bg-card` + `--shadow-modal` | Playfair + Lora + Outfit | Gold icon + confetti | All three screens now share the same warm editorial card pattern. ## Implementation Order 1. `home.css` — Profile editor CSS overhaul (highest impact) 2. `Home.jsx` — Title, placeholders, username feedback 3. `welcomemessage.css` — Full rewrite 4. `WelcomeMessage.jsx` — SVG, CTA, copy ## Verification 1. Start the dev server (`npm run dev` in client/) 2. Create a new account via `/signUp` 3. Verify the profile setup modal matches the editorial design (warm bg, gold focus rings, Playfair title) 4. Verify username field shows green "Available" / red "Taken" with icons 5. After saving, verify welcome message has pen icon, gold confetti, "Begin writing" CTA 6. Toggle dark mode and verify all three screens adapt properly 7. Test at mobile width (< 480px) — modal should be ~92% width If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: C:\Users\johnm\.claude\projects\C--Users-johnm-iskrib-v3\fe80371b-3929-42ef-84c9-a8cc9a204773.jsonl

file.content:
# Onboarding Flow Design Overhaul ## Context The signup/login pages have a polished editorial design (warm cream, gold accents, Playfair Display typography, morphing blobs). But the **profile setup modal** uses a cold dark navy (`rgb(4,20,29)`) with no CSS variables, and the **welcome message** is a plain white card with a generic party popper SVG. These three consecutive screens feel like three different apps. This plan harmonizes them into one cohesive editorial flow. ## Files to Modify | File | Change | |------|--------| | `client/src/components/HomePage/home.css` | Restyle profile editor: replace hardcoded colors with CSS variables, match auth page design | | `client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx` | Update title/copy, add placeholders, color-coded username feedback | | `client/src/components/WelcomeMessage/welcomemessage.css` | Full rewrite: CSS variables, dark mode, remove duplicate @media blocks | | `client/src/components/WelcomeMessage/WelcomeMessage.jsx` | Replace party popper with pen icon, add "Begin writing" CTA, update copy | ## Change 1: Profile Setup Modal (home.css + Home.jsx) ### CSS — Replace hardcoded navy with warm editorial theme - `.profile-editor`: `rgb(4,20,29)` -> `var(--bg-card)` + `var(--border-card)` + `var(--shadow-modal)`. Change from `width:50%; height:90%` to `max-width:440px; height:auto` (matches auth-form-card pattern) - `.profile-editor-bg`: `rgba(141,141,141,0.2)` -> `var(--bg-backdrop-heavy)`, increase blur to 8px - `.edit-profile-title-container`: Add `font-family: "Playfair Display"`, use `var(--text-heading)` - `.save-bttn` / `.save-bttn-disabled`: Match `.primary-button` from signup (dark bg, hover lift, disabled opacity) - `.edit-profile-name-input` / `.edit-profile-bio-input`: Match `.auth-input` from signup — `var(--bg-primary)`, `var(--border-card)`, gold focus ring (`box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(212,168,83,0.12)`) - `.input-title`: Match label style from signup — uppercase, `var(--text-muted)`, `0.7rem` - `.input-title-container`: Change to `justify-content: space-between` (label left, counter right) - `.name-input-container` / `.bio-input-container`: Remove border wrapper, use gap layout - `.profile-edit-container`: Remove hardcoded navy background - Add `@keyframes slideUp` (same as signup.css) for the card entrance - Remove hardcoded `color: rgb(255,255,255) !important` from mobile @media blocks - Add username status classes: `.username-status-available` (green), `.username-status-taken` (red), `.username-status-checking` (muted) - Add dark mode overrides for elevated inputs ### JSX — Welcoming copy + username feedback - Title: "Edit profile" -> "Welcome to Iskrib" - Add subtitle: `

Set up your writing identity.

` - Add `placeholder="Your name"` on name input - Add `placeholder="A few words about you..."` on bio textarea - Username status: apply color classes + prepend checkmark/X unicode chars - Save button: show "Saving..." text during upload, disable during upload ## Change 2: Welcome Message (welcomemessage.css + WelcomeMessage.jsx) ### CSS — Full rewrite - `.welcome-message-container`: `position: fixed`, `var(--bg-backdrop-heavy)`, blur 6px - `.welcome-message-child-container`: `var(--bg-card)`, `var(--border-card)`, `var(--shadow-modal)`, `max-width: 400px`, `border-radius: 16px` - Remove the 54px square close icon -> small 32px circular close button top-right (`.welcome-close-btn`) - Add `.welcome-heading`: Playfair Display, `var(--text-heading)` - `.welcome-message`: Lora serif, italic, `var(--text-body)` - `.iskrib-word`: Playfair Display, `var(--accent-amber)`, bold - Add `.welcome-cta`: Full-width button matching `.primary-button` (dark bg, hover lift) - Add `.welcome-icon`: `var(--accent-amber)` for the pen icon - Dark mode: gold CTA button, stronger shadow - **Remove duplicate @media blocks** (lines 56-77 and 79-100 were identical) - Single responsive block at 480px ### JSX — Editorial design - Replace 80px party popper SVG (100+ lines) with 48px Material pen/edit icon (single path, `currentColor`) - Add heading: `

You're all set

` - Update message: "Your thoughts now have a home on **Iskrib**. The blank page awaits." - Add CTA: `` (calls `onClose`) - Add small X close button top-right (aria-labeled) - Confetti colors: brand palette `['#D4A853', '#C4943E', '#E0BA6A', '#FAF9F6', '#8A9E7A']` instead of rainbow - Softer animation: `scale: 0.95, y: 12` initial instead of `scale: 0` (gentle reveal vs popping from nothing) ## Visual Consistency After Changes | Screen | Background | Card | Typography | Accent | |--------|-----------|------|------------|--------| | Signup | `--bg-primary` | `--bg-card` + `--shadow-card-sm` | Playfair + Lora + Outfit | Gold focus rings | | Profile Setup | `--bg-backdrop-heavy` | `--bg-card` + `--shadow-modal` | Playfair + Lora + Outfit | Gold focus rings | | Welcome | `--bg-backdrop-heavy` | `--bg-card` + `--shadow-modal` | Playfair + Lora + Outfit | Gold icon + confetti | All three screens now share the same warm editorial card pattern. ## Implementation Order 1. `home.css` — Profile editor CSS overhaul (highest impact) 2. `Home.jsx` — Title, placeholders, username feedback 3. `welcomemessage.css` — Full rewrite 4. `WelcomeMessage.jsx` — SVG, CTA, copy ## Verification 1. Start the dev server (`npm run dev` in client/) 2. Create a new account via `/signUp` 3. Verify the profile setup modal matches the editorial design (warm bg, gold focus rings, Playfair title) 4. Verify username field shows green "Available" / red "Taken" with icons 5. After saving, verify welcome message has pen icon, gold confetti, "Begin writing" CTA 6. Toggle dark mode and verify all three screens adapt properly 7. Test at mobile width (< 480px) — modal should be ~92% width

1→-- Constellations feature: user-created links between stars in the Universe view
     2→
     3→-- 1. Create constellations table
     4→CREATE TABLE constellations (
     5→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
     6→    star_id_a UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES journals(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
     7→    star_id_b UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES journals(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
     8→    user_id_a UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
     9→    user_id_b UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    10→    label TEXT DEFAULT '',
    11→    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'accepted', 'declined')),
    12→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    13→    CONSTRAINT different_users CHECK (user_id_a != user_id_b),
    14→    CONSTRAINT different_stars CHECK (star_id_a != star_id_b),
    15→    CONSTRAINT unique_star_pair UNIQUE (star_id_a, star_id_b)
    16→);
    17→
    18→CREATE INDEX idx_const_star_a ON constellations(star_id_a);
    19→CREATE INDEX idx_const_star_b ON constellations(star_id_b);
    20→CREATE INDEX idx_const_status ON constellations(status);
    21→
    22→-- 2. Add constellation_id to notifications table
    23→ALTER TABLE notifications
    24→ADD COLUMN constellation_id UUID REFERENCES constellations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE DEFAULT NULL;
    25→
    26→-- 3. RPC for viewport constellation fetch
    27→DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS get_viewport_constellations(UUID[]);
    28→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION get_viewport_constellations(post_ids UUID[])
    29→RETURNS TABLE (
    30→    id UUID, star_id_a UUID, star_id_b UUID,
    31→    user_id_a UUID, user_id_b UUID,
    32→    label TEXT, status TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    33→    a_universe_x REAL, a_universe_y REAL,
    34→    b_universe_x REAL, b_universe_y REAL,
    35→    a_title TEXT, b_title TEXT
    36→) AS $$
    37→BEGIN
    38→    RETURN QUERY
    39→    SELECT c.id, c.star_id_a, c.star_id_b, c.user_id_a, c.user_id_b,
    40→           c.label, c.status, c.created_at,
    41→           ja.universe_x AS a_universe_x, ja.universe_y AS a_universe_y,
    42→           jb.universe_x AS b_universe_x, jb.universe_y AS b_universe_y,
    43→           ja.title::TEXT AS a_title, jb.title::TEXT AS b_title
    44→    FROM constellations c
    45→    JOIN journals ja ON ja.id = c.star_id_a
    46→    JOIN journals jb ON jb.id = c.star_id_b
    47→    WHERE c.status IN ('accepted', 'pending')
    48→      AND (c.star_id_a = ANY(post_ids) OR c.star_id_b = ANY(post_ids));
    49→END;
    50→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql STABLE;
    51→

1→-- Galaxy Gravity System: Embedding-based spatial clustering
     2→-- Run this in the Supabase SQL editor
     3→-- Safe to re-run (uses DROP IF EXISTS + IF NOT EXISTS)
     4→
     5→-- 1. Galaxy centroids table
     6→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.galaxy_centroids (
     7→    user_id UUID PRIMARY KEY REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
     8→    mean_embedding vector(384),
     9→    galaxy_x FLOAT,
    10→    galaxy_y FLOAT,
    11→    post_count INT DEFAULT 0,
    12→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()
    13→);
    14→
    15→-- 2. Galaxy similarity edges
    16→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS public.galaxy_edges (
    17→    user_a UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    18→    user_b UUID REFERENCES auth.users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    19→    similarity FLOAT NOT NULL,
    20→    PRIMARY KEY (user_a, user_b),
    21→    CHECK (user_a < user_b)
    22→);
    23→
    24→-- 3. Settled star positions on journals
    25→ALTER TABLE public.journals
    26→ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS settled_x FLOAT,
    27→ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS settled_y FLOAT;
    28→
    29→-- 4. Index for viewport queries on settled coords
    30→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS journals_settled_coords_idx
    31→ON public.journals (settled_x, settled_y)
    32→WHERE settled_x IS NOT NULL AND settled_y IS NOT NULL AND privacy = 'public';
    33→
    34→-- 5. RPC: viewport query (includes both display and original universe coords)
    35→DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.get_universe_posts_viewport;
    36→CREATE FUNCTION public.get_universe_posts_viewport(
    37→    vp_min_x FLOAT, vp_max_x FLOAT,
    38→    vp_min_y FLOAT, vp_max_y FLOAT,
    39→    max_count INT DEFAULT 200
    40→)
    41→RETURNS TABLE (
    42→    id UUID, title TEXT, content TEXT, post_type TEXT,
    43→    canvas_doc JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    44→    display_x FLOAT, display_y FLOAT,
    45→    universe_x FLOAT, universe_y FLOAT,
    46→    user_id UUID, user_name TEXT, user_image_url TEXT, user_badge TEXT,
    47→    like_count BIGINT
    48→)
    49→LANGUAGE sql STABLE AS $$
    50→    SELECT j.id, j.title, j.content, j.post_type, j.canvas_doc, j.created_at,
    51→           COALESCE(j.settled_x, j.universe_x) AS display_x,
    52→           COALESCE(j.settled_y, j.universe_y) AS display_y,
    53→           j.universe_x, j.universe_y,
    54→           u.id, u.name, u.image_url, u.badge,
    55→           COALESCE(lc.cnt, 0)
    56→    FROM public.journals j
    57→    LEFT JOIN public.users u ON u.id = j.user_id
    58→    LEFT JOIN LATERAL (SELECT count(*) AS cnt FROM public.likes WHERE journal_id = j.id) lc ON true
    59→    WHERE j.privacy = 'public'
    60→      AND COALESCE(j.settled_x, j.universe_x) BETWEEN vp_min_x AND vp_max_x
    61→      AND COALESCE(j.settled_y, j.universe_y) BETWEEN vp_min_y AND vp_max_y
    62→    ORDER BY j.created_at DESC
    63→    LIMIT GREATEST(max_count, 1);
    64→$$;
    65→
    66→-- 6. RPC: find similar post pairs for semantic bridges
    67→DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.find_similar_post_pairs;
    68→CREATE FUNCTION public.find_similar_post_pairs(
    69→    post_ids UUID[],
    70→    threshold FLOAT DEFAULT 0.9,
    71→    max_pairs INT DEFAULT 50
    72→)
    73→RETURNS TABLE (post_a UUID, post_b UUID, similarity FLOAT)
    74→LANGUAGE sql STABLE AS $$
    75→    SELECT a.id, b.id, 1 - (a.embeddings <=> b.embeddings)
    76→    FROM public.journals a
    77→    JOIN public.journals b ON b.id > a.id
    78→    WHERE a.id = ANY(post_ids) AND b.id = ANY(post_ids)
    79→      AND a.embeddings IS NOT NULL AND b.embeddings IS NOT NULL
    80→      AND 1 - (a.embeddings <=> b.embeddings) >= threshold
    81→    ORDER BY 3 DESC
    82→    LIMIT max_pairs;
    83→$$;
    84→
    85→-- 7. RPC: compute star-to-galaxy pulls for visible posts
    86→DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.get_star_galaxy_pulls;
    87→CREATE FUNCTION public.get_star_galaxy_pulls(
    88→    post_ids UUID[],
    89→    min_similarity FLOAT DEFAULT 0.4,
    90→    max_pulls INT DEFAULT 500
    91→)
    92→RETURNS TABLE (post_id UUID, galaxy_user_id UUID, similarity FLOAT)
    93→LANGUAGE sql STABLE AS $$
    94→    SELECT j.id, gc.user_id, 1 - (j.embeddings <=> gc.mean_embedding)
    95→    FROM public.journals j
    96→    CROSS JOIN public.galaxy_centroids gc
    97→    WHERE j.id = ANY(post_ids)
    98→      AND j.embeddings IS NOT NULL
    99→      AND gc.mean_embedding IS NOT NULL
   100→      AND j.user_id != gc.user_id
   101→      AND 1 - (j.embeddings <=> gc.mean_embedding) >= min_similarity
   102→    ORDER BY 3 DESC
   103→    LIMIT max_pulls;
   104→$$;
   105→
   106→-- 8. RPC: find wormhole pairs (semantically identical posts far apart)
   107→DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS public.find_wormhole_pairs;
   108→CREATE FUNCTION public.find_wormhole_pairs(
   109→    similarity_threshold FLOAT DEFAULT 0.85,
   110→    min_distance FLOAT DEFAULT 0,
   111→    max_pairs INT DEFAULT 15
   112→)
   113→RETURNS TABLE (
   114→    post_a UUID, post_b UUID, similarity FLOAT,
   115→    a_x FLOAT, a_y FLOAT, b_x FLOAT, b_y FLOAT,
   116→    a_title TEXT, b_title TEXT
   117→)
   118→LANGUAGE sql STABLE
   119→SECURITY DEFINER
   120→AS $$
   121→    SELECT DISTINCT ON (LEAST(a.id, b.id), GREATEST(a.id, b.id))
   122→        a.id, b.id,
   123→        1 - (a.embeddings <=> b.embeddings),
   124→        COALESCE(a.settled_x, a.universe_x),
   125→        COALESCE(a.settled_y, a.universe_y),
   126→        COALESCE(b.settled_x, b.universe_x),
   127→        COALESCE(b.settled_y, b.universe_y),
   128→        a.title, b.title
   129→    FROM public.journals a
   130→    CROSS JOIN LATERAL (
   131→        SELECT j.id, j.title, j.user_id, j.embeddings,
   132→               j.settled_x, j.settled_y, j.universe_x, j.universe_y
   133→        FROM public.journals j
   134→        WHERE j.privacy = 'public'
   135→          AND j.embeddings IS NOT NULL
   136→          AND j.user_id != a.user_id
   137→        ORDER BY j.embeddings <=> a.embeddings
   138→        LIMIT 5
   139→    ) b
   140→    WHERE a.privacy = 'public'
   141→      AND a.embeddings IS NOT NULL
   142→      AND 1 - (a.embeddings <=> b.embeddings) >= similarity_threshold
   143→    ORDER BY LEAST(a.id, b.id), GREATEST(a.id, b.id),
   144→             1 - (a.embeddings <=> b.embeddings) DESC
   145→    LIMIT max_pairs;
   146→$$;

### **2. SERVICES**

#### `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/server/services/constellationService.js` (206 lines)
**Exports:**
- `requestConstellationService(senderId, starIdA, starIdB, label)` - Creates constellation request notification
- `respondConstellationService(userId, constellationId, accept)` - Updates constellation status (accepted/declined)
- `getViewportConstellationsService(postIds)` - Fetches visible constellations via RPC
- `deleteConstellationService(userId, constellationId)` - Deletes constellation

#### `/c/Users/johnm/iskrib_v3/server/services/galaxySettleService.js` (599 lines)
**Purpose:** Force-directed graph layout system for positioning users and posts in universe view

**Exports:**
- `recomputeAllCentroids()` - Computes mean embeddings for all users with public posts
- `recomputeGalaxyEdges()` - Calculates similarity between user centroids (threshold 0.25)
- `settleGalaxyPositions()` - Force-directed layout for galaxy centroids

|---|---|

- **Line 3**: Remove `respondConstellation` from the import

- **Line 6**: Remove `import { incrementalSettleForUser } from "./galaxySettleService.js"`

- **Lines 349–360**: Remove the universe coordinate computation block (the `postCount` query + `userHomeCoords` + `spiralLayout` + setting `universe_x`/`universe_y`)

## Supabase SQL to DROP

Run this in the Supabase SQL Editor. The order matters (foreign keys first).

```sql
ALTER TABLE journals DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS settled_x;
ALTER TABLE journals DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS settled_y;

```

3. Delete standalone server files (constellationController, constellationService, galaxySettleService, scripts)
4. Edit shared server files (routes.js, getController.js, getService.js, uploadService.js)
5. Provide SQL migration for the user to run in Supabase

## Verification

1. `npm run dev` in `client/` — app starts, no import errors

4. Create a new post — upload succeeds without universe coord errors
5. Open notifications — constellation notifications render gracefully (empty or hidden, no crash)
6. Run SQL in Supabase SQL Editor — tables and columns dropped cleanly

# Onboarding Flow Design Overhaul ## Context The signup/login pages have a polished editorial design (warm cream, gold accents, Playfair Display typography, morphing blobs). But the **profile setup modal** uses a cold dark navy (`rgb(4,20,29)`) with no CSS variables, and the **welcome message** is a plain white card with a generic party popper SVG. These three consecutive screens feel like three different apps. This plan harmonizes them into one cohesive editorial flow. ## Files to Modify | File | Change | |------|--------| | `client/src/components/HomePage/home.css` | Restyle profile editor: replace hardcoded colors with CSS variables, match auth page design | | `client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx` | Update title/copy, add placeholders, color-coded username feedback | | `client/src/components/WelcomeMessage/welcomemessage.css` | Full rewrite: CSS variables, dark mode, remove duplicate @media blocks | | `client/src/components/WelcomeMessage/WelcomeMessage.jsx` | Replace party popper with pen icon, add "Begin writing" CTA, update copy | ## Change 1: Profile Setup Modal (home.css + Home.jsx) ### CSS — Replace hardcoded navy with warm editorial theme - `.profile-editor`: `rgb(4,20,29)` -> `var(--bg-card)` + `var(--border-card)` + `var(--shadow-modal)`. Change from `width:50%; height:90%` to `max-width:440px; height:auto` (matches auth-form-card pattern) - `.profile-editor-bg`: `rgba(141,141,141,0.2)` -> `var(--bg-backdrop-heavy)`, increase blur to 8px - `.edit-profile-title-container`: Add `font-family: "Playfair Display"`, use `var(--text-heading)` - `.save-bttn` / `.save-bttn-disabled`: Match `.primary-button` from signup (dark bg, hover lift, disabled opacity) - `.edit-profile-name-input` / `.edit-profile-bio-input`: Match `.auth-input` from signup — `var(--bg-primary)`, `var(--border-card)`, gold focus ring (`box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(212,168,83,0.12)`) - `.input-title`: Match label style from signup — uppercase, `var(--text-muted)`, `0.7rem` - `.input-title-container`: Change to `justify-content: space-between` (label left, counter right) - `.name-input-container` / `.bio-input-container`: Remove border wrapper, use gap layout - `.profile-edit-container`: Remove hardcoded navy background - Add `@keyframes slideUp` (same as signup.css) for the card entrance - Remove hardcoded `color: rgb(255,255,255) !important` from mobile @media blocks - Add username status classes: `.username-status-available` (green), `.username-status-taken` (red), `.username-status-checking` (muted) - Add dark mode overrides for elevated inputs ### JSX — Welcoming copy + username feedback - Title: "Edit profile" -> "Welcome to Iskrib" - Add subtitle: `

` - Add `placeholder="Your name"` on name input - Add `placeholder="A few words about you..."` on bio textarea - Username status: apply color classes + prepend checkmark/X unicode chars - Save button: show "Saving..." text during upload, disable during upload ## Change 2: Welcome Message (welcomemessage.css + WelcomeMessage.jsx) ### CSS — Full rewrite - `.welcome-message-container`: `position: fixed`, `var(--bg-backdrop-heavy)`, blur 6px - `.welcome-message-child-container`: `var(--bg-card)`, `var(--border-card)`, `var(--shadow-modal)`, `max-width: 400px`, `border-radius: 16px` - Remove the 54px square close icon -> small 32px circular close button top-right (`.welcome-close-btn`) - Add `.welcome-heading`: Playfair Display, `var(--text-heading)` - `.welcome-message`: Lora serif, italic, `var(--text-body)` - `.iskrib-word`: Playfair Display, `var(--accent-amber)`, bold - Add `.welcome-cta`: Full-width button matching `.primary-button` (dark bg, hover lift) - Add `.welcome-icon`: `var(--accent-amber)` for the pen icon - Dark mode: gold CTA button, stronger shadow - **Remove duplicate @media blocks** (lines 56-77 and 79-100 were identical) - Single responsive block at 480px ### JSX — Editorial design - Replace 80px party popper SVG (100+ lines) with 48px Material pen/edit icon (single path, `currentColor`) - Add heading: `

# Remove Universe Feature

## Context

Removing the Universe page and all related code (constellations, galaxy settle, star positioning). The feature is largely self-contained, but has tendrils in a few shared files (uploadService, routes, notifications, nav links) that need surgical cleanup.

## Files to DELETE entirely

| File / Folder | What it is |
|---|---|
| `client/src/components/Universe/` | Entire folder — 27 files (component, layers, hooks, utils, CSS module) |
| `server/controller/constellationController.js` | Constellation CRUD controller |
| `server/services/constellationService.js` | Constellation DB service |
| `server/services/galaxySettleService.js` | Force-directed graph layout engine |
| `server/scripts/runGalaxySettle.js` | Galaxy settle CLI script |
| `server/scripts/backfillUniverseCoords.js` | Backfill universe_x/y script |
| `server/sql/constellations.sql` | Constellation table DDL (reference only) |
| `server/sql/galaxy_gravity.sql` | Galaxy tables/RPCs DDL (reference only) |

## Files to EDIT (surgical removals)

### 1. `client/src/App.jsx`
- **Line 37**: Remove `const Universe = lazy(...)` import
- **Line 156**: Remove `<Route path='/universe' element={<Universe/>}/>`

### 2. `client/src/components/HomePage/Home.jsx`
- **Lines 94–111**: Remove the `universeLink` object
- **Lines 199–200**: Remove `universeLink` from the `links` arrays (both guest and auth)

### 3. `client/src/components/mobileNavLink/MobileNavLink.jsx`
- **Lines 64–83**: Remove the Universe nav link object from `navLinks` array

### 4. `client/src/components/ProfilePage/constants/profileSidebarLinks.jsx`
- **Lines 35–52**: Remove the Universe link object from the returned array

### 5. `client/API/Api.js`
- Remove 5 functions (~lines 1115–1205): `requestConstellation`, `respondConstellation`, `getViewportConstellations`, `deleteConstellation`, `getUniversePosts`

### 6. `client/src/utils/useMutation.js`
- **Line 3**: Remove `respondConstellation` from the import
- **Lines 422–455**: Remove entire `useRespondConstellationMutation` export

### 7. `client/src/components/Notifications/notificationsCards.jsx`
- **Line 12**: Remove `useRespondConstellationMutation` from import
- **Lines 80–92**: Remove `constellation_request` and `constellation_accepted` icon objects from `iconArray`
- **Lines 335–384**: Remove the constellation notification onClick handler and constellation display blocks. The `constellation_request`/`constellation_accepted` cases should fall through to a no-op or generic "notification unavailable" instead of crashing (these notification types may still exist in the DB). Simplest approach: let them render nothing — they'll fall to the `: null` default naturally once the JSX branches are removed.
- Remove `mutationRespondConstellation` hook call (find and remove the line that calls `useRespondConstellationMutation`)

### 8. `server/routes/routes.js`
- **Line 15**: Remove constellation controller import
- **Line 12**: Remove `getUniversePostsController` from the getController import
- **Lines 102, 113**: Remove `constellation_id` and `constellations!constellation_id(...)` from `NOTIFICATION_JOURNAL_SELECT` string
- **Line 272**: Remove `router.get('/universe/posts', ...)` route
- **Lines 1317–1320**: Remove all 4 constellation routes

### 9. `server/controller/getController.js`
- **Line 1**: Remove `getUniversePostsService` from the import
- **Lines 172–182**: Remove `getUniversePostsController` export

### 10. `server/services/getService.js`
- Remove `getUniversePostsService` export and all universe helper functions: `normalizeUniversePost`, `loadUniversePostsViaRpc`, `loadUniversePostsFallback`, `getUniverseCentroids`, `getUniverseEdges`, `getUniverseSimilarPairs`, `getUniverseStarPulls`

### 11. `server/services/uploadService.js`
- **Line 6**: Remove `import { incrementalSettleForUser } from "./galaxySettleService.js"`
- **Lines 11–33**: Remove `simpleHash`, `userHomeCoords`, `spiralLayout` helper functions
- **Lines 349–360**: Remove the universe coordinate computation block (the `postCount` query + `userHomeCoords` + `spiralLayout` + setting `universe_x`/`universe_y`)
- **Lines 386–393**: Remove the `incrementalSettleForUser` call block

## Supabase SQL to DROP

Run this in the Supabase SQL Editor. The order matters (foreign keys first).

```sql
-- 1. Drop RPC functions that reference these tables
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS get_viewport_constellations(uuid[]);
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS get_universe_posts_viewport(double precision, double precision, double precision, double precision, integer);
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS find_similar_post_pairs(uuid[], double precision, integer);
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS get_star_galaxy_pulls(uuid[], double precision, integer);
DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS find_wormhole_pairs(double precision, double precision, integer);

-- 2. Drop constellation table (it has FK to journals and users)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS constellations;

-- 3. Drop galaxy tables
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS galaxy_edges;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS galaxy_centroids;

-- 4. Drop the index on settled coords
DROP INDEX IF EXISTS journals_settled_coords_idx;

-- 5. Remove universe/settle columns from journals table
ALTER TABLE journals DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS universe_x;
ALTER TABLE journals DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS universe_y;
ALTER TABLE journals DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS settled_x;
ALTER TABLE journals DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS settled_y;

-- 6. Remove constellation_id column from notifications table
ALTER TABLE notifications DROP COLUMN IF EXISTS constellation_id;
```

## Implementation Order

1. Delete the `client/src/components/Universe/` folder
2. Edit all client files (App.jsx, Home.jsx, MobileNavLink.jsx, profileSidebarLinks.jsx, Api.js, useMutation.js, notificationsCards.jsx)
3. Delete standalone server files (constellationController, constellationService, galaxySettleService, scripts)
4. Edit shared server files (routes.js, getController.js, getService.js, uploadService.js)
5. Provide SQL migration for the user to run in Supabase

## Verification

1. `npm run dev` in `client/` — app starts, no import errors
2. Navigate sidebar links — no Universe link in desktop sidebar, mobile nav, or profile sidebar
3. `/universe` route returns 404 / blank (no crash)
4. Create a new post — upload succeeds without universe coord errors
5. Open notifications — constellation notifications render gracefully (empty or hidden, no crash)
6. Run SQL in Supabase SQL Editor — tables and columns dropped cleanly
